Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidateInputParameters
Step Action: Open the DB Shrink Application design document and confirm the required input parameters are defined: ClientName and ShrinkDatabase flag
Step Expected Result: Input parameters ClientName and ShrinkDatabase are documented with valid data types and accepted values

Step Action: Confirm that the ClientName parameter maps to a valid tenant identifier in the environment
Step Expected Result: ClientName resolves to the correct tenant schema prefix with no ambiguity

Step Action: Confirm that ShrinkDatabase is a boolean flag and that both true and false states are handled by the application
Step Expected Result: Both values are handled with documented behavior for each state

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidatePhase1_PrepareStagingData
Step Action: Execute Phase 1 of the DB Shrink process against the target tenant database in a non-production environment
Step Expected Result: Phase 1 completes without error and staging tables are created with correct structure

Step Action: Verify that all staging tables are populated with the expected row counts before proceeding to Phase 2
Step Expected Result: Staging table row counts match source data counts within the documented tolerance

Step Action: Confirm that no production data is modified during Phase 1
Step Expected Result: Production tables remain unchanged; all changes are isolated to staging objects

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidatePhase2_DisableConstraints
Step Action: Execute Phase 2 to disable foreign key constraints on the target tables
Step Expected Result: Phase 2 completes and all listed foreign key constraints are disabled without error

Step Action: Query the database to confirm constraints are in a disabled state before Phase 3 runs
Step Expected Result: sys.foreign_keys or equivalent view shows is_disabled = 1 for all targeted constraints

Step Action: Confirm that constraint disable scope is limited to the tables defined in the design doc
Step Expected Result: No additional constraints outside the defined scope are affected

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidatePhase3_TruncateData
Step Action: Execute Phase 3 to truncate historical data from the target tables
Step Expected Result: Phase 3 completes and row counts in target tables are reduced to the expected post-truncation values

Step Action: Confirm that reference/lookup tables and active records are not truncated
Step Expected Result: Protected tables retain all rows; only archival data is removed

Step Action: Verify that the truncation is logged and the before/after row counts are captured
Step Expected Result: Log file or audit table records pre- and post-truncation counts for each affected table

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidatePhase4_RestoreBatchedData
Step Action: Execute Phase 4 to restore batched data from staging into the target tables
Step Expected Result: Phase 4 completes and retained records are reinserted without duplicate key or constraint violations

Step Action: Confirm that batch inserts complete within the documented time window
Step Expected Result: Phase 4 execution time falls within the expected range defined in the design doc

Step Action: Verify that row counts in the target tables after Phase 4 match the expected retained data volume
Step Expected Result: Post-restore row counts equal the count of records that met the retention criteria

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidatePhase5_RecreateConstraints
Step Action: Execute Phase 5 to re-enable and recreate foreign key constraints
Step Expected Result: Phase 5 completes and all constraints are re-enabled without validation errors

Step Action: Run a database integrity check (DBCC CHECKCONSTRAINTS or equivalent) after Phase 5
Step Expected Result: Integrity check returns no violations; all relationships are valid

Step Action: Confirm that the database is fully operational and accessible to the Windward application after Phase 5
Step Expected Result: Application login and basic data retrieval succeed against the shrunk database

Title: GD_DBShrink_ValidateEndToEndOnNonProd
Step Action: Execute the full 5-phase DB Shrink process end-to-end on a copy of the production database in a non-production environment
Step Expected Result: All 5 phases complete successfully and the resulting database is smaller than the source

Step Action: Compare the shrunk database size to the target size documented in the design doc
Step Expected Result: Final database size meets or is less than the documented target size

Step Action: Perform a functional smoke test against the shrunk database using the Playwright suite
Step Expected Result: Playwright suite passes all tests confirming the application works correctly with shrunk data

Title: GD_ActiveDirectory_ValidateDomainStrategyDecision
Step Action: Review the Active Directory decision document and confirm that the chosen strategy (on-prem DC
Step Expected Result: AWS DC
column4: or AWS AD service) is documented with rationale
column5: The selected AD strategy is documented with justification and trade-offs acknowledged

Step Action: Confirm that the decision aligns with the InfoSec tenant isolation requirements
Step Expected Result: The AD configuration supports per-tenant identity isolation as described in the security discovery notes

Step Action: Verify that the AD strategy decision has been signed off by the relevant stakeholders
Step Expected Result: Sign-off or approval record exists for the AD strategy

Title: GD_ActiveDirectory_ValidateConnectivity
Step Action: Confirm that the Windward application servers can reach the Active Directory domain controller (on-prem or AWS) on required ports (389 LDAP / 636 LDAPS / 88 Kerberos)
Step Expected Result: Port connectivity tests pass from each Windward Web and App server to the domain controller

Step Action: Perform a domain join test for a Windward server in the target AWS environment
Step Expected Result: Server successfully joins the domain and appears in the correct OU

Step Action: Verify that Group Policy Objects (GPOs) apply correctly to the joined server
Step Expected Result: GPO application succeeds and required security policies are enforced on the server

Title: GD_AmazonConnect_ValidateTenantAlignment
Step Action: Confirm that the Amazon Connect instance is provisioned under the correct AWS account and region for the target tenant
Step Expected Result: Amazon Connect instance exists in us-east-1 under the correct tenant account

Step Action: Verify that the Evolt CX dependency for Amazon Connect has been provisioned and is reachable
Step Expected Result: Evolt CX endpoints are accessible and respond to health checks

Step Action: Confirm that call routing in Amazon Connect is configured for the new tenant with correct queue and flow definitions
Step Expected Result: Amazon Connect contact flows route calls to the correct tenant queues with no misrouted calls during test

Title: GD_AmazonConnect_ValidateIntegrationWithWindward
Step Action: Place a test call through Amazon Connect and verify that the call context (caller ID / member ID) is passed to the Windward application
Step Expected Result: Windward receives the call context data and can display the correct member record

Step Action: Confirm that CTI screen-pop functionality works for an inbound call in the test environment
Step Expected Result: Screen-pop displays the correct member information within the documented SLA time

Step Action: Verify that call wrap-up data is written back to the Windward database after call completion
Step Expected Result: Call record is created in the Windward database with the correct disposition and duration

Title: GD_BizTalk_ValidateEndpointDesign
Step Action: Confirm that the BizTalk EDI endpoint design document identifies all inbound and outbound EDI transaction sets for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: All required EDI transaction sets (837
column6: etc.) are listed with direction and trading partner

Step Action: Review the on-prem vs cloud EDI strategy decision and confirm it is documented with a clear recommendation
Step Expected Result: Strategy document exists with a chosen approach and associated risks

Step Action: Verify that the BizTalk receive and send port configurations are templated and repeatable for a new tenant onboarding
Step Expected Result: Port configuration template exists and can be applied without manual customization per tenant

Title: GD_BizTalk_ValidateEDITransactionFlow
Step Action: Send a test EDI 837 transaction through the BizTalk pipeline and confirm it is processed without errors
Step Expected Result: 837 message processes successfully and a corresponding record is created in the Windward claims database

Step Action: Send a test EDI 270 eligibility inquiry and confirm a valid 271 response is returned
Step Expected Result: 271 response is returned within the documented SLA and contains correct eligibility data

Step Action: Confirm that EDI transaction failures are routed to the BizTalk suspended message queue and alerted
Step Expected Result: Failed messages appear in the suspended queue and an alert is generated within the expected time

Title: GD_CitrixLB_ValidateALBNLBReplacement
Step Action: Confirm that the Citrix Load Balancer has been replaced by AWS ALB (for HTTP/HTTPS) and AWS NLB (for TCP) for the Windward 1.0 tier
Step Expected Result: AWS ALB and NLB are created and configured; Citrix LB is no longer in the traffic path

Step Action: Verify that ALB listener rules route requests to the correct Windward Web target group
Step Expected Result: HTTP/HTTPS requests reach the correct Windward Web servers as confirmed by access logs

Step Action: Verify that NLB routes TCP traffic to the correct Windward App server target group
Step Expected Result: TCP connections reach the correct Windward App servers as confirmed by connection logs

Title: GD_CitrixLB_ValidateDNSStrategy
Step Action: Confirm that DNS records (Route 53 or on-prem DNS) resolve the Windward application hostname to the correct ALB/NLB endpoint
Step Expected Result: nslookup or dig against the Windward hostname returns the correct ALB/NLB DNS name

Step Action: Verify that the DNS TTL values are set appropriately to support failover without long propagation delays
Step Expected Result: DNS TTL is set to the value documented in the DNS strategy; failover DNS change propagates within that TTL

Step Action: Confirm that split-horizon DNS is configured correctly if on-prem and cloud DNS are both in use
Step Expected Result: On-prem clients resolve to the on-prem endpoint; cloud clients resolve to the AWS endpoint

Title: GD_CitrixVDI_ValidateLatencyBaseline
Step Action: Document the baseline latency from the VDI environment (Citrix NetScaler) to the Windward 1.0 application servers
Step Expected Result: Baseline latency measurements are recorded in milliseconds from VDI endpoint to Windward Web server

Step Action: Compare VDI-to-cloud latency against the documented acceptable threshold
Step Expected Result: Measured latency is at or below the documented threshold (e.g. < 50 ms round-trip)

Step Action: Confirm that VDI users can log into Windward 1.0 via the cloud-hosted path without session timeouts during the latency test
Step Expected Result: VDI session remains stable for the duration of the 15-minute sustained latency test with no disconnections

Title: GD_BIReporting_ValidatePowerBIDataPlatform
Step Action: Confirm that the Power BI data platform connection is configured to point to the correct read-only replica for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Power BI data source connection string targets the read-only replica endpoint and not the primary

Step Action: Run a sample Power BI report and verify that data returns correctly for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Report renders with correct tenant data and row counts match the source database

Step Action: Confirm that the BI Enterprise Reporting module is accessible to authorized users in the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Authorized users can log in to the BI reporting portal and view tenant-specific reports; no cross-tenant data is visible

Title: GD_BIReporting_ValidateDataRefreshSchedule
Step Action: Confirm that the Power BI dataset refresh schedule is configured and active for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Dataset refresh schedule is visible in Power BI Service and shows last successful refresh timestamp

Step Action: Trigger a manual refresh and verify it completes without error
Step Expected Result: Manual refresh completes within the documented time window with no failures

Step Action: Verify that the refresh failure alerting is configured and sends notification to the correct distribution list
Step Expected Result: Simulated refresh failure generates an alert to the documented recipients within 15 minutes

Title: GD_Delphix_ValidateDeMasking
Step Action: Confirm that the Delphix de-identification process has been executed for the new tenant data set prior to use in non-production environments
Step Expected Result: Delphix job completion record exists and shows successful masking of all sensitive columns

Step Action: Spot-check a sample of masked records and verify that PII fields (SSN
Step Expected Result: DOB
column4: name) are replaced with synthetic data
column5: Selected records show masked values in PII columns; no real PII is present in the non-production copy

Step Action: Confirm that the masked data set is functionally valid for use by the Windward application
Step Expected Result: Windward application can load and display masked member records without errors

Title: GD_Delphix_ValidateMaskingScope
Step Action: Review the Delphix masking ruleset and confirm all tables containing PHI/PII are included in the masking definition
Step Expected Result: Masking ruleset covers all tables identified in the data classification inventory

Step Action: Confirm that referential integrity is maintained after masking (foreign keys between masked tables remain valid)
Step Expected Result: Database integrity check passes on the masked dataset; no orphaned foreign key references

Step Action: Verify that the Delphix process can be re-run repeatably for future tenant data refreshes
Step Expected Result: Re-run of the Delphix job on a refreshed data set completes successfully and produces a newly masked copy

Title: GD_ElectronicEligibility_ValidateAPIChoice
Step Action: Confirm that the Electronic Eligibility (EE) vs Member API data flow decision is documented and the chosen integration path is defined for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Decision document exists specifying whether EE or Member API is used and the rationale

Step Action: Verify that the chosen API endpoint is reachable from the Windward application server
Step Expected Result: API health check returns HTTP 200 from the Windward App server to the chosen eligibility endpoint

Step Action: Confirm that eligibility inquiry responses are returned within the documented SLA
Step Expected Result: Test eligibility query returns a valid response within the documented time (e.g. < 3 seconds)

Title: GD_ElectronicEligibility_ValidateDataFlow
Step Action: Submit a test eligibility inquiry for a known member and verify the response contains correct coverage information
Step Expected Result: Response contains correct plan
column5: and eligibility dates matching the source system record

Step Action: Confirm that the EE transaction is logged in the Windward database with the correct status
Step Expected Result: Eligibility transaction log record exists with member ID

Step Action: Verify that a failed eligibility response is handled gracefully by the Windward application
Step Expected Result: Application displays a user-friendly error message and does not crash when eligibility service is unavailable

Title: GD_CloudMgmtGateway_ValidateVDIAccess
Step Action: Confirm that the Cloud Management Gateway (CMG) is configured to allow VDI-based access to the Windward 1.0 application
Step Expected Result: CMG configuration document shows Windward 1.0 as an approved application accessible via VDI

Step Action: Log into the Windward 1.0 application through the VDI session using a test account
Step Expected Result: Login succeeds and the Windward home screen loads within the acceptable latency threshold

Step Action: Confirm that clipboard and file transfer policies are enforced within the VDI session per InfoSec requirements
Step Expected Result: VDI session enforces data transfer restrictions; clipboard paste from local to VDI is blocked if required by policy

Title: GD_InfoSec_ValidateIAMRoleProvisioning
Step Action: Confirm that IAM roles for the new tenant are provisioned following the documented tenant-based security model
Step Expected Result: IAM roles exist in the correct AWS account with the documented permission boundaries

Step Action: Verify that cross-tenant IAM access is not possible by testing role assumption from one tenant account to another
Step Expected Result: Attempt to assume a role from a different tenant account returns AccessDenied

Step Action: Confirm that IAM roles are tagged with the correct tenant identifier
Step Expected Result: All tenant IAM roles have a Tenant tag matching the client name

Title: GD_InfoSec_ValidateTenantSecurityIsolation
Step Action: Confirm that all AWS resources for the new tenant are tagged with the tenant identifier and reside within the tenant VPC
Step Expected Result: All EC2
column5: and Lambda resources are tagged correctly and are within the designated tenant VPC

Step Action: Verify that VPC security groups do not allow inbound access from other tenant VPCs
Step Expected Result: Security group rules for the tenant VPC have no inbound rules referencing CIDR ranges of other tenant VPCs

Step Action: Confirm that network ACLs enforce the documented ingress and egress rules for the tenant VPC
Step Expected Result: Network ACL rules match the documented firewall policy for the tenant tier

Title: GD_InfoSec_ValidateMonitoringAndAlerts
Step Action: Confirm that CloudWatch log groups are created for all Windward application tiers and are actively receiving logs
Step Expected Result: CloudWatch log groups exist for Web
column5: and DB tiers; log streams show recent entries

Step Action: Verify that Exabeam is receiving log events from all Windward servers in the new tenant environment
Step Expected Result: Exabeam shows active log sources for each Windward server; no source is in a stale state

Step Action: Confirm that a test alert (simulated login failure spike) triggers within the expected detection window
Step Expected Result: Simulated brute-force event generates an Exabeam alert within the documented SLA

Title: GD_Okta_ValidateOAGInstanceInAWS
Step Action: Confirm that the Okta Access Gateway (OAG) instance is deployed in the AWS environment for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: OAG instance is running in the correct AWS account and is reachable from Windward application servers

Step Action: Verify that the Windward application SSO flow redirects to Okta and returns a valid SAML assertion
Step Expected Result: Initiating login from Windward redirects to the Okta login page; successful authentication returns SAML assertion to Windward

Step Action: Confirm that the Okta application assignment for Windward is scoped to the correct user groups for the new tenant
Step Expected Result: Only users in the designated Okta groups can access the Windward application; unauthorized users receive an access denied response

Title: GD_Okta_ValidateFastpassAuthenticator
Step Action: Confirm that Okta Fastpass is configured as a phishing-resistant authenticator for Windward users
Step Expected Result: Okta policy for the Windward application requires Fastpass or FIDO2; password-only login is blocked

Step Action: Enroll a test user with Okta Fastpass and authenticate to the Windward application
Step Expected Result: Test user completes Fastpass enrollment and successfully authenticates to Windward without entering a password

Step Action: Attempt to authenticate with a phishing-simulated credential and confirm Fastpass blocks the attempt
Step Expected Result: Fastpass rejects the simulated phishing credential and does not grant access

Title: GD_DLP_ValidateFingerprintingForNewTenant
Step Action: Confirm that a DLP fingerprint has been created for the new tenant data set prior to going live
Step Expected Result: DLP fingerprint record exists in the DLP management console for the new tenant

Step Action: Verify that the DLP fingerprint is associated with the correct business unit and data classification level
Step Expected Result: Fingerprint metadata shows the correct tenant name

Step Action: Confirm that a test document containing synthetic tenant data is detected by DLP scanning
Step Expected Result: DLP scan flags the test document as a policy violation; the alert is routed to the correct DLP admin queue

Title: GD_SQLClustering_ValidateCluster1
Step Action: Confirm that SQL Cluster 1 (SQL01 + SQL02) is provisioned with the correct Windows Server Failover Clustering (WSFC) configuration
Step Expected Result: Failover Cluster Manager shows SQL01 and SQL02 as cluster nodes in an online state

Step Action: Confirm that a manual failover of Cluster 1 from SQL01 to SQL02 completes within the documented RTO
Step Expected Result: Failover completes within the documented time; SQL02 becomes the active node and applications reconnect automatically

Title: GD_SQLClustering_ValidateCluster2

Step Expected Result: Failover Cluster Manager shows SQL03 and SQL04 as cluster nodes in an online state

Step Action: Verify that Cluster 2 has the correct number of listener IPs configured (2 listeners as per design)
Step Expected Result: Two cluster listeners are created with the documented IP addresses and port 1433

Step Action: Confirm that a manual failover of Cluster 2 from SQL03 to SQL04 completes within the documented RTO
Step Expected Result: Failover completes within the documented time; SQL04 becomes the active node and applications reconnect automatically

Title: GD_SecurityTools_ValidateInstallationOrder
Step Action: Confirm that the documented security tool installation order is followed: Beyond Trust → SentinelOne → Qualys → Imperva → Forcepoint → Exabeam (and Illumio last for DB servers)
Step Expected Result: Build runbook shows each tool installed in the correct sequence with timestamps recorded

Step Action: Verify that each security tool is active and reporting after installation by checking its management console
Step Expected Result: Management console for each tool shows the server as online and reporting within 15 minutes of installation

Step Action: Confirm that installing the tools in a different order causes a known dependency failure by reviewing the documented rationale
Step Expected Result: Dependency chain documentation explains why order matters (e.g. Beyond Trust must be installed before EDR enrollment)

Title: GD_SecurityTools_ValidateBeyondTrustAccess

Step Action: Verify that an administrator can access a Windward server via Beyond Trust without requiring direct RDP credentials

Step Action: Confirm that Beyond Trust session recording is enabled for all server access in the new tenant

Title: GD_ServerAliases_ValidateRequiredAliases

Step Expected Result: Application configuration files reference alias names; connectivity tests using the alias succeed for each service

Step Action: Confirm that removing or changing an alias causes a documented and detectable failure mode
Step Expected Result: Alias dependency is documented; removal of a test alias causes the expected connection failure in the application log

Title: GD_CorrespondenceRouter_ValidateArchitecture

Step Action: Confirm that the Correspondence Router logs all routing decisions for the new tenant
